_comment: IDSA clinical practice guidelines. Source: IDSA 2022/2023 guidelines.
_structure: syndrome -> organism -> {first_line, dose, duration, notes, alternatives}

K. pneumoniae (susceptible).notes: Standard gram-negative bacteremia. Switch to oral if improving at day 5.

K. pneumoniae (ESBL).notes: ESBL-producing organism. Carbapenems are drug of choice.

K. pneumoniae (CRE).notes: Carbapenem-resistant Enterobacterales. Confirm no MBL before using.

E. coli (susceptible).notes: Susceptible E. coli bloodstream infection. De-escalate to the narrowest active agent once stable.

E. coli (ESBL).notes: ESBL-producing E. coli bacteremia. Carbapenem therapy is preferred for invasive infection.

E. coli (MDR).notes: Carbapenem-resistant E. coli (CRE). Confirm no metallo-beta-lactamase before using ceftazidime-avibactam.

P. aeruginosa (susceptible).notes: Use an active antipseudomonal beta-lactam and narrow based on susceptibility.

P. aeruginosa (resistant).notes: MDR Pseudomonas resistant to standard agents. Use ceftazidime-avibactam if susceptible; confirm with susceptibility testing.

P. aeruginosa (MDR).notes: XDR Pseudomonas. Combination therapy often required. Infectious disease consult strongly recommended.

S. aureus (MSSA).duration: 14-42 days depending on source and complications
S. aureus (MSSA).notes: MSSA bacteremia should receive an anti-staphylococcal beta-lactam when tolerated.

S. aureus (MRSA).notes: MRSA bacteremia. Add rifampin only if prosthetic material involved.

S. aureus (MDR).notes: VISA/hVISA or daptomycin-non-susceptible MRSA. Use high-dose daptomycin. Consult ID. Combination with beta-lactam may restore susceptibility.

Enterococcus (VSE).notes: Vancomycin-susceptible, ampicillin-susceptible Enterococcus. Ampicillin is preferred when susceptible.

Enterococcus (VRE).notes: Vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus. Use linezolid or daptomycin based on syndrome and susceptibility.

Enterococcus (MDR).notes: Pan-drug-resistant Enterococcus. High-dose daptomycin with ampicillin synergy or linezolid. Infectious disease consult required.

E. coli (susceptible).notes: Complicated UTI or pyelonephritis. Step down to active oral therapy when clinically stable.

E. coli (ESBL).notes: ESBL UTI — carbapenems for complicated infection or pyelonephritis.

E. coli (MDR).notes: Carbapenem-resistant E. coli UTI. Confirm susceptibility. Cefiderocol is an alternative if ceftazidime-avibactam MIC is high.

K. pneumoniae (susceptible).notes: Susceptible Klebsiella complicated UTI. Narrow therapy once susceptibility is known.

K. pneumoniae (ESBL).notes: ESBL Klebsiella UTI — oral step-down to trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole or fosfomycin if susceptible after stabilization.

K. pneumoniae (CRE).notes: CRE complicated UTI. Confirm susceptibility and avoid inactive carbapenems.

P. aeruginosa (susceptible).notes: Pseudomonal UTI typically requires anti-pseudomonal beta-lactam.

P. aeruginosa (resistant).notes: MDR Pseudomonas UTI resistant to standard agents. Confirm susceptibility before using.

P. aeruginosa (MDR).notes: XDR Pseudomonas UTI. Combination therapy may be needed. ID consult required.

S. aureus (MSSA).notes: MSSA UTI — anti-staphylococcal beta-lactam preferred. Consider source (catheter, stones) and rule out bacteremia.

S. aureus (MDR).notes: Vancomycin-intermediate or resistant S. aureus UTI. Linezolid has good urinary penetration. ID consult recommended.

Enterococcus (VSE).notes: VSE UTI — ampicillin if susceptible. Step down to oral amoxicillin.

Enterococcus (MDR).dose: 3g PO once (cystitis) or IV formulation for complicated UTI
Enterococcus (MDR).notes: Pan-drug-resistant Enterococcus UTI. Fosfomycin or nitrofurantoin for lower UTI if susceptible. ID consult for upper UTI.

S. aureus (MRSA).notes: MRSA pneumonia. Linezolid is a common alternative; do not use daptomycin for pneumonia.

S. aureus (MSSA).notes: MSSA pneumonia — anti-staphylococcal beta-lactam preferred over vancomycin.

S. aureus (MDR).notes: VISA/hVISA pneumonia. Linezolid preferred over vancomycin for MDR S. aureus pneumonia due to better lung penetration. Do NOT use daptomycin.

P. aeruginosa (susceptible).notes: HAP/VAP due to susceptible Pseudomonas. Avoid daptomycin (inactivated by lung surfactant).

P. aeruginosa (resistant).notes: MDR Pseudomonas HAP/VAP. Ceftolozane-tazobactam has superior lung penetration vs. ceftazidime-avibactam. Confirm susceptibility.

P. aeruginosa (MDR).notes: XDR Pseudomonas pneumonia. Combination therapy may be needed. Inhaled colistin as adjunct for VAP. ID consult required.

K. pneumoniae (susceptible).notes: Susceptible Klebsiella pneumonia. De-escalate from broad empiric therapy when possible.

K. pneumoniae (ESBL).notes: ESBL Klebsiella pneumonia — carbapenem required. Meropenem for severe/ICU cases.

E. coli (susceptible).notes: Gram-negative pneumonia — adjust based on susceptibility report.

E. coli (ESBL).notes: ESBL E. coli pneumonia — carbapenem required. E. coli pneumonia is uncommon; rule out aspiration and alternative diagnoses.

E. coli (MDR).notes: CRE E. coli pneumonia — rare. Confirm susceptibility. Combination therapy for severe cases.

Enterococcus (VRE).notes: VRE pneumonia — linezolid preferred due to lung penetration.

Enterococcus (MDR).notes: Pan-resistant Enterococcal pneumonia — extremely rare. Linezolid is usually retained. ID consult mandatory.

E. coli (susceptible).notes: Complicated intra-abdominal infection needs gram-negative and anaerobic coverage plus source control.

E. coli (ESBL).notes: ESBL IAI — ertapenem preferred over meropenem for non-critical cases (stewardship).

E. coli (MDR).notes: CRE E. coli IAI. Add metronidazole for anaerobic coverage. Source control is paramount.

K. pneumoniae (susceptible).notes: Susceptible Klebsiella intra-abdominal infection. Maintain anaerobic coverage.

K. pneumoniae (ESBL).notes: ESBL Klebsiella IAI — ertapenem provides both gram-negative and anaerobic coverage.

K. pneumoniae (CRE).notes: CRE IAI — needs active coverage. Source control critical.

P. aeruginosa (susceptible).notes: Hospital-acquired IAI or prior antibiotic exposure — cover Pseudomonas.

P. aeruginosa (resistant).notes: MDR Pseudomonas IAI. Add metronidazole for anaerobic coverage. Confirm susceptibility.

P. aeruginosa (MDR).notes: XDR Pseudomonas IAI. Add metronidazole for anaerobic coverage. Source control mandatory. ID consult.

S. aureus (MSSA).notes: MSSA IAI — usually post-surgical. Add metronidazole or ensure coverage of anaerobes with combined regimen.

S. aureus (MRSA).notes: MRSA IAI — uncommon, usually post-surgical. Combine with anaerobic coverage.

S. aureus (MDR).notes: VISA/hVISA IAI — linezolid or high-dose daptomycin. Add anaerobic coverage. ID consult.

Enterococcus (VSE).notes: Covers susceptible Enterococcus faecalis plus gram-negative and anaerobic intra-abdominal flora.

Enterococcus (VRE).notes: VRE IAI — rare. Ensure source control. Daptomycin alternative.

Enterococcus (MDR).notes: Pan-resistant Enterococcus IAI. Linezolid usually retained. High-dose daptomycin with ampicillin as alternative. ID consult mandatory.
